reasoning: Pete Hegseth survived a contentious confirmation process in January 2025 and faced a major scandal in late March when it was revealed he discussed military plans in a Signal group chat that accidentally included a journalist. Despite calls for his resignation from Democrats and some Republicans, and the launch of a DoD Inspector General probe on April 3, President Trump and the White House have forcefully defended him, characterizing the backlash as a "witch hunt." Under the Trump administration, officials who retain the President's backing rarely leave solely due to media or congressional pressure. Additionally, Hegseth has continued actively executing his agenda (e.g., initiating widespread civilian Pentagon firings in early April). Given that the Polymarket estimated a 10% probability on April 3—right after the peak of the scandal—and no major negative developments have occurred since, the likelihood of him departing before June 30 under these conditions is quite low. I grant a slightly higher probability (13%) to account for potential unexpected findings from the IG investigation or typical unpredictable turnover in the administration.
